preview_text:
The model rates Saudi Arabia as the slight favorite with a 40% win probability, while Cape Verde has a 29% chance of victory. The draw sits at 31%, indicating genuine competitive balance underpinning the matchup. Saudi Arabia's edge is modest rather than decisive.

The spread across outcomes reveals a closely contested match. No single result dominates the probability distribution; the gap between favorite and underdog is only 11 percentage points. A draw emerges as a plausible outcome at nearly one-third likelihood, suggesting the teams are expected to be well-matched in a neutral setting.

The model and market diverge meaningfully on favorite identification. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both favor Cape Verde at 36%, a gap of 7 percentage points above the model's 29%. Conversely, the market rates Saudi Arabia's win probability 5 percentage points lower than the model does. Draw probabilities align closely across sources. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these framings, but the discrepancy warrants attention when making decisions against either estimate.
